biomassSource: Jenkins, J.C., Chojnacky, D.C., Heath, L.S., Birdsey, R.A. (2003). National-scale biomass estimators for United States tree species. Forest Science 49(1):12-35. Equation: ln(biomass_kg) = β0 + β1 × ln(DBH_cm).
carbonFractionSource: IPCC (2006) Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Vol 4, Table 4.3. Broadleaf: 0.47, Conifer: 0.51.
interceptionSource: Rao, M., et al. (2014). USDA National Urban Forest Effects model (UFORE/i-Tree Eco). Regional averages for NE US deciduous canopy.

hardwood-deciduous._note: Applies to most NJ street trees: maples, oaks, planes, elms, zelkova, linden, etc.
hardwood-deciduous.source: Jenkins et al. 2003 hardwood, IPCC Tier 1 carbon fraction

softwood-evergreen._note: Pines, spruces, firs. Less common as NJ street trees.
softwood-evergreen.source: Jenkins et al. 2003 softwood, IPCC Tier 1 conifer carbon fraction

small-ornamental._note: Small-stature ornamentals: serviceberry, dogwood, callery pear, etc.
small-ornamental.source: Jenkins et al. 2003 hardwood with conservative coefficients for small-stature forms

_genericFallbackNote: Any species not listed above uses the hardwood-deciduous coefficients. This is a conservative choice appropriate for most NJ broadleaf street trees.
